title: Standardized Error Envelope for All API Error Responses
description: All API error responses shall use a standardized error envelope containing a success flag, machine-readable error code, user-friendly message, optional details array, unique request ID, and server timestamp.
principle: The frontend shall never need to interpret arbitrary backend error formats.

title: HTTP Status Codes Map to Defined Error Categories
description: The API shall use standard HTTP status codes to distinguish validation, authorization, resource, business-rule, integration, rate-limit, timeout, and server errors.

httpStatusMapping.useCase: Gemini or external API rate limit

title: Field-Level Validation Errors Identify Affected Field, Code, and User-Facing Message
description: Field-level validation errors shall identify the affected field, machine-readable validation code, and user-friendly message. The React UI maps error.details[].field to inline form field errors.

details.message: Plan year must be between 2026 and 2035.

title: Centralized Frontend Error Handler Based on HTTP Status and Error Code
description: The client application shall provide centralized error handling based on HTTP status and machine-readable error codes. The handler shall route each error type to the appropriate UI response.

frontendErrorRoutingTable.uiPattern: AI error banner + Retry + document safe
Continue Waiting / Run in Background options

title: Unique Request ID in Every API Response for Troubleshooting and Audit Correlation
description: Every API response — both success and error — shall include a unique request ID for troubleshooting and audit trail correlation.

title: Internal Server Errors Never Expose Stack Traces, API Keys, or Infrastructure Details
description: Internal server errors shall not expose stack traces, API keys, infrastructure details, or sensitive information in client-facing API responses. Detailed error information shall be logged server-side only.

error.message: Something went wrong while processing your request.

uiDisplay: Something went wrong. Please try again. Reference ID: REQ-ERR-10045

title: Retryable Errors Identify Whether the Operation Can Be Safely Retried
description: Retryable errors shall include a retryable flag in the error details. Non-retryable errors shall not prompt the user to retry an operation that will fail again.

title: Rate-Limited AI Operations Include Retry Metadata
description: Rate-limited AI operations shall include retry metadata in the error response. The UI shall display a non-technical user message and handle retry automatically without exposing HTTP 429 or RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED to the user.

error.message: The AI service is temporarily busy. Please try again.

uiDisplay: AI Service Temporarily Busy — we'll retry automatically. Retry attempt 1 of 3.

title: Integration Errors Include Transaction-Level Error Information
description: Carrier and payroll integration errors shall include transaction-level error information where available, enabling the user to navigate directly to the specific failing transaction.

error.message: The carrier rejected the enrollment transmission.

title: All API Errors Logged Server-Side with Request ID for End-to-End Traceability
description: API errors shall be logged server-side with document ID (where applicable), job ID (where applicable), attempt number, error code, and request ID. Logs shall not contain SSN, API keys, or other sensitive values.
